title: Metcalfia mexicana
intro: Metcalfia mexicana is a type of plant in the family Melanthiaceae, commonly known as the "Mexican Mayapple" or "Wild Mayapple", native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Metcalfia mexicana include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include tinctures and infusions, often used to treat minor ailments such as skin irritations and digestive issues.

The plant's roots and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Metcalfia mexicana is also used to treat gastrointestinal disorders and to reduce inflammation.
